document.title: Guidelines on Substances used as Processing Aids
document.label: CXG 75-2010 - Guidelines on Substances used as Processing Aids

sections.title: OBJECTIVES AND SCOPE
sections.text: The Guidelines aim to provide information for the safe use of substances used as processing aids and the safety of their residues in the preparation of foods and food ingredients.
sections.retrieval_text:
OBJECTIVES AND SCOPE
number=1
level=1
The Guidelines aim to provide information for the safe use of substances used as processing aids and the safety of their residues in the preparation of foods and food ingredients.

page_spans.text: The Guidelines aim to provide information for the safe use of substances used as processing aids and the safety of their residues in the preparation of foods and food ingredients.

section_path_titles: OBJECTIVES AND SCOPE

sections.title: DEFINITION
sections.text: Processing aid means any substance or material, not including apparatus or utensils, and not consumed as a food ingredient by itself, intentionally used in the processing of raw materials, foods or its ingredients, to fulfil a certain technological purpose during treatment or processing and which may result in the non-intentional but unavoidable presence of residues or derivatives in the final product.

level=1

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: Labelling of substances used as processing aids should be in accordance with the requirement of the Codex General Standard for Labelling of Food Additives When Sold as Such (CODEX STAN 107-1981) and the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Food (CODEX STAN 1-1985).

sections.title: The use of a substance as a processing aid is justified when such use performs one or more technological functions during treatment or processing of raw materials, foods, or ingredients. Any residues of processing aids remaining in the food after processing should not perform a technological function in the final product.

source.process: THE USE OF A SUBSTANCE AS A PROCESSING AID IS JUSTIFIED WHEN SUCH USE PERFORMS ONE OR MORE TECHNOLOGICAL FUNCTIONS DURING TREATMENT OR PROCESSING OF RAW MATERIALS, FOODS, OR INGREDIENTS. ANY RESIDUES OF PROCESSING AIDS REMAINING IN THE FOOD AFTER PROCESSING SHOULD NOT PERFORM A TECHNOLOGICAL FUNCTION IN THE FINAL PRODUCT.

sections.title: Substances used as processing aids shall be used under conditions of good manufacturing practices (GMP) which includes the following:
sections.parent_title: PRINCIPLES FOR THE SAFE USE OF SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS
path_titles: PRINCIPLES FOR THE SAFE USE OF SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS
sections.text: • The quantity of the substance used shall be limited to the lowest achievable level necessary to accomplish its desired technological function; • Residues or derivatives of the substance remaining in food should be reduced to the extent reasonably achievable and should not pose any health risk; and • The substance is prepared and handled in the same way as a food ingredient.

sections.title: Substances used as processing aids should be of food grade quality.
sections.parent_title: PRINCIPLES FOR THE SAFE USE OF SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS
path_titles: PRINCIPLES FOR THE SAFE USE OF SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS
sections.text: This can be demonstrated by conforming to the applicable specifications of identity and purity recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission or, in the absence of such a specification, with an appropriate specification developed by responsible national or international bodies or suppliers.

sections.title: Substances used as processing aids should comply with any applicable microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997) and should be prepared and handled in accordance with the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1-1969) and other relevant Codex texts.

classification.sentence: The Guidelines aim to provide information for the safe use of substances used as processing aids and the safety of their residues in the preparation of foods and food ingredients.
classification.pattern: Declarative sentence stating purpose and scope

components.purpose: provide information for the safe use of substances used as processing aids
components.safety_aspect: safety of their residues in the preparation of foods and food ingredients

classification.justification: The sentence is a general statement of purpose without normative modality or condition; it defines scope and aim, fitting an informative category with a declarative pattern.
notes: No normative or directive modality present; purely descriptive.

sentences.original: This can be demonstrated by conforming to the applicable specifications of identity and purity recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission or, in the absence of such a specification, with an appropriate specification developed by responsible national or international bodies or suppliers.

prefilter.reason: Describes compliance methods with standards, relevant for regulatory context.

classification.sentence: This can be demonstrated by conforming to the applicable specifications of identity and purity recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission or, in the absence of such a specification, with an appropriate specification developed by responsible national or international bodies or suppliers.
classification.pattern: Modal phrase 'can be demonstrated by' + specification conformity conditions

components.demonstration_method: conforming to the applicable specifications of identity and purity recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission or appropriate alternative specifications

classification.justification: The sentence describes a permitted way to demonstrate compliance; modality is permissive with illustrative function; partial template match to permission types.

pages.title: GUIDELINES ON SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS
pages.summary: A página estrutura o documento com o título principal e cinco seções, numeradas 1, 2, 3, e 5. A seção 3 possui subseções 3.1 a 3.5 explicando princípios para o uso seguro. A seção 3 continua na página seguinte. Níveis hierárquicos e textos estão claros, e o cabeçalho foi excluído do texto dos nós.
